Monday: Margaret opened with "Jalapeño corn dog bites. We're doing it this week and I need to know right..." - questioning whether the dish had real technique behind it. Marcus confirmed the cornmeal-jalapeño structure was legitimate. Stephanie proposed leading with the moisture-seal angle, which Margaret approved. Marcus will anchor copy around how the cornmeal crust concentrates heat on the bite.

Tuesday: Margaret confirmed a 60-40 cornmeal-to-flour minimum after testing - "Marcus gets it. Fine. Cornmeal-to-flour ratio is 60-40 minimum or the seal breaks down in the oil." Marcus framed this as a performance threshold, not a limitation. Stephanie agreed the copy should anchor on crust performance rather than ingredients. All three aligned on shipping.

Wednesday: Julian presented three options for the bite-through shot. Stephanie pushed for shot two - the crisp exterior shattering - saying "Shot two's the one - if people are stopping their scroll, it's because they see that crisp exterior breaking apart, right?" Margaret agreed, prioritizing the crisp over the jalapeno cross-section Julian had championed. Shot two locked in.

Thursday: Marcus brought finalized copy focusing on cornmeal's moisture-seal function. Margaret pushed to lead with the steam-absorption mechanic - not taste. Stephanie flagged repetition: "Love that you went all in on the moisture-seal thing - let's see what you've got?" Marcus tightened the structure to lead with cornmeal arresting moisture, then pivot to texture. Margaret approved - cut the explanation, show the fry result.

Friday: Margaret opened with "Final review. Shot two locked, copy locked. Someone tell me what isn't right, because something..." - expecting problems. Marcus pushed back, saying trusting the work improved it. Stephanie credited the team listening over defending positions. Marcus and Julian agreed restraint drove both the copy and shot two - science-first writing and minimal angles were the winning approach.

Saturday: This conversation contains no discussion of "Jalapeno Corn Dog Bites" - it is a technical deployment exchange. Devon confirmed verification passed and pushed the project live. Margaret advocated for restraint as a guiding principle, which Devon agreed with. No ingredients, techniques, or food ideas were discussed.
